perturbation.original_text: Ready4S and Client are sometimes collectively referred to herein as the ” Parties” and individually as a ” Party”. A Party includes the entity named herein, its successors and assigns, any of its present or future corporate parents, subsidiaries, and organizations controlled by, controlling or under common control with such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Ready4S and Client are sometimes collectively referred to herein as the ” Parties”. An Individual party includes the entity named herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines "A Party" very broadly to include successors, assigns, parents, subsidiaries, etc. The modified text redefines it as "An Individual party" and includes only the named entity. This creates an internal contradiction. Whereever the definition of party comes into place, it will raise some confusion whether it is reffering to the new definition or the old.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ready4S and Client are sometimes collectively referred to herein as the ” Parties”. An Individual party includes the entity named herein. A Party includes the entity named herein, its successors and assigns, any of its present or future corporate parents, subsidiaries, and organizations controlled by, controlling or under common control with such Party. Each of the Parties may be the ” Disclosing Party ” and the other party will be deemed to be the ” Receiving Party ”.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'A Party' broadly, including successors and subsidiaries. The changed text redefines it as 'An Individual party' and includes only the named entity. The contradicted text includes both the new and old definitions of the party which creates a direct contradiction. The modified definition directly conflicts with the original definition which exists in the contradicted text, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosing Party means the Party whose Conﬁdential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party Personnel means any and all staﬀ, employees, directors, oﬃcers and professional advisors of a Party Purpose means any and all discussions and negotiations relating to a potential agreement between the Parties hereto as more particularly set out in Recital (A) Receiving Party means the Party who is in receipt of Conﬁdential Information
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing Party means the Party whose Conﬁdential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party Workforce means any and all personnel of a Party. Purpose means any and all discussions and negotiations relating to a potential agreement between the Parties hereto as more particularly set out in Recital (A) Receiving Party means the Party who is in receipt of Conﬁdential Information
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses "Personnel" to define who are the people involved with the parties. The modified text uses "Workforce" instead of "Personnel". Then when referencing workforce it will become confusing since the legal text has already established a different term which "Personnel".
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Receiving Party shall only disclose or reveal any Conﬁdential Information disclosed to it to those of its Personnel who are required in the course of their duties to receive and consider the same in so far as is necessary to fulﬁl the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Personnel' as staff, employees, directors, officers, and professional advisors. The changed text replaces 'Personnel' with 'Workforce'. The contradicted text refers to 'Personnel' in the context of disclosing confidential information. By changing 'Personnel' to 'Workforce' in the definition section, the contradicted text now references a term that is no longer defined as encompassing staff, employees, directors, officers and professional advisors causing confusion and contradiction. This creates a clear and direct conflict since the contradicted text now uses a term that has no legal meaning within the context of the changed section, and the definition of 'Personnel' is what is assumed in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Prior to the disclosure of any Conﬁdential Information to any such Personnel, the Receiving Party shall inform them of the conﬁdential nature of the material and of the provisions of this conﬁdentiality undertaking and obtain written conﬁrmation of their acceptance of these terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Prior to the disclosure of any Conﬁdential Information to any such Workforce, the Receiving Party shall inform them of the conﬁdential nature of the material and of the provisions of this conﬁdentiality undertaking and obtain verbal conﬁrmation of their acceptance of these terms.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires "written confirmation" from the Personnel about the acceptance of these terms. The modified text changes it to require only "verbal confirmation". This creates a contradiction regarding the type of confirmation needed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prior to the disclosure of any Conﬁdential Information to any such Workforce, the Receiving Party shall inform them of the conﬁdential nature of the material and of the provisions of this conﬁdentiality undertaking and obtain verbal conﬁrmation of their acceptance of these terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text required 'written confirmation,' while the changed text mandates only 'verbal confirmation.' This directly contradicts any section, including the contradicted text, that requires or assumes written confirmation, as highlighted in the explanation. The change introduces conflicting requirements regarding the type of confirmation needed, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, immediately upon receipt of Notice to that eﬀect: (a) return to the Disclosing Party all Conﬁdential Information (including all copies held); (b) destroy those portions (and all copies) of any analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by it for its use containing or reﬂecting or generated from, in whole or in part, any Conﬁdential Information; and (c) expunge and destroy any Conﬁdential Information from any computer, word processor or other device in its possession or custody or control containing such information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, immediately upon receipt of Notification to that eﬀect: (a) return to the Disclosing Party all Conﬁdential Information (including all copies held); (b) destroy those portions (and all copies) of any analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by it for its use containing or reﬂecting or generated from, in whole or in part, any Conﬁdential Information; and (c) expunge and destroy any Conﬁdential Information from any computer, word processor or other device in its possession or custody or control containing such information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses the term "Notice" which is reffered to section 11. The modified text changes the word from "Notice" to "Notification", there is no defintion or context to what notification means or any implied or express terms to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, immediately upon receipt of Notification to that eﬀect: (a) return to the Disclosing Party all Conﬁdential Information (including all copies held); (b) destroy those portions (and all copies) of any analyses, compilations, studies, reports or other documents prepared by it for its use containing or reﬂecting or generated from, in whole or in part, any Conﬁdential Information; and (c) expunge and destroy any Conﬁdential Information from any computer, word processor or other device in its possession or custody or control containing such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Notice" with "Notification". The explanation states that "Notice" is defined in section 11, while "Notification" has no definition, leading to a misalignment of terminology. The contradicted text also uses "Notification". This means that the changed text introduces a term without definition, causing the contradicted text (which refers to this undefined term) to be ambiguous, creating a clear and impactful contradiction as defined by the Misaligned Terminology definition.
